## Ground rules:
- Follow the user's instructions. 
- DO NOT wrap answers in markdown code blocks, which means no triple backticks like ``` nor single backtick like ` .
- Do not explain answers, just give me code. 
- If changing existing code, preserve unrelated code and comments. 
- Never add comments to the end of a line. 
- Never add stupid comments.
- Be considerate with indentation. 
- Prefer readable code over of comments. 
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

FYI, here is my current buffer in Neovim. Use this as context for my request.
```lua/ask-openai/predictions/context/prompts.tests.lua

local prompts = require("ask-openai.predictions.context.prompts")
local skills = require("ask-openai.frontends.skills")

require('ask-openai.helpers.testing')

describe("parse_includes", function()
    describe("/all", function()
        local function should_detect_slash_all(original_prompt, expected_cleaned)
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes(original_prompt)
            assert.is_true(includes.all, "includes.all should be true for '" .. original_prompt .. "'")
            assert.are_equal(expected_cleaned, includes.rendered_prompt)
        end
        it("'/all' slash command is detected and stripped", function()
            should_detect_slash_all("foo /all bar", "foo bar")
            should_detect_slash_all("/all foo", "foo")
            should_detect_slash_all("foo /all", "foo")
        end)

        local function should_not_detect_slash_all(original_prompt)
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes(original_prompt)
            assert.is_false(includes.all, "includes.all should be false for '" .. original_prompt .. "'")
            assert.are_equal(original_prompt, includes.rendered_prompt)
        end
        it("'/all' alone is not detected - for now b/c that means theres no user prompt!", function()
            -- FYI maybe I want to have this parse and strip? but for now it seems moot to do this without any other prompt text
            should_not_detect_slash_all("/all")
        end)
        it("'/allFoo' - in front of word is not stripped", function()
            should_not_detect_slash_all("/allFoo ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" /allFoo ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" /allFoo")
            should_not_detect_slash_all("/allFoo")
        end)
        it("'foo/allbar' - in between word is not stripped", function()
            should_not_detect_slash_all("foo/allbar ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" foo/allbar ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" foo/allbar")
            should_not_detect_slash_all("foo/allbar")
        end)
        it("'bar/all' - at end of word is not stripped", function()
            should_not_detect_slash_all("bar/all ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" bar/all ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" bar/all")
            should_not_detect_slash_all("bar/all")
        end)
    end)
    describe("/others", function()
        it("should detect /yanks", function()
            function ensure_detects(command, field)
                field = field or command
                local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. command .. " bar")
                assert.is_true(includes[field], "includes." .. field .. " should be true")
                assert.are_equal("foo bar", includes.rendered_prompt)
            end

            -- just add one test per, do not exercise tests of the parsing/stripping
            ensure_detects("yanks")
            ensure_detects("commits")
            ensure_detects("file", "current_file")
            ensure_detects("WIP_open_files", "open_files")
            ensure_detects("selection", "include_selection")
        end)
    end)

    describe("skills", function()
        local fake_skill_name = "fake_skilly_poo"
        skills.cached_skill_commands = { fake_skill_name }
        -- skills._skill_paths[skill_name] = "/fake/skilly/poo" -- not required if injecting content into cache

        it("should detect and load skill commands", function()
            skills._skill_content_cache[fake_skill_name] = "INJECTED SKILLY POO"
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. fake_skill_name .. " bar")
            assert.are_equal("foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO", includes.rendered_prompt)
        end)

        it("should recursively resolve static slash commands within skill content", function()
            skills._skill_content_cache[fake_skill_name] = "INJECTED SKILLY POO /all"
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. fake_skill_name .. " bar")
            assert.is_true(includes.all, "includes.all should be true due to /all in skill content")
            assert.are_equal("foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO", includes.rendered_prompt)
            -- TODO test of all slash command embedded (LOOP?)
        end)

        -- TODO test of embedded top_k
    end)
end)

```

## General Code Preferences

- When rewriting code, leave unrelated code and unrelated comments as is.
- When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example:
```lua
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## Lua Code Preferences

Naming
- Use snake_case for functions and variables.
- Use PascalCase for classes.

Type Hints
- Prefer EmmyLua/LuaLS style annotations
- Always annotate when type inference would be ambiguous, or for public APIs.

Examples:
```lua
---@param names string[]
---@param checker fun(name: string): boolean
---@return table<string, integer>
function process_names(names, checker) end

---@class WindowController
---@field window hs.axuielement
---@field title? string
---@field size { w: number, h: number }
local WindowController = {}
```

## ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/predictions/context/prompts.lua:90-170
---@param prompt? string
---@return ParseIncludesResult
function M.parse_includes(prompt)
    prompt = prompt or ""

    -- Extract /k=<number> first, before other processing
    local top_k, prompt_without_k = extract_top_k(prompt)
    prompt = prompt_without_k

    -- Process skill slash commands first: detect skill references, load their content,
    -- resolve any built‑in slash commands inside the skill content, and clean the
    -- skill content before injecting it.
    local raw_skill_contents = {}
    local rendered_prompt = prompt
    for _, skill_name in pairs(skills.get_skill_commands()) do
        local cmd = "/" .. skill_name
        -- Detect presence of the skill command using the same pattern logic as `has`
        local found = rendered_prompt:find("%W" .. cmd .. "%W")
        found = found or rendered_prompt:find("^" .. cmd .. "%W")
        found = found or rendered_prompt:find("%W" .. cmd .. "$")
        if found then
            -- Remove the skill reference from the prompt
            rendered_prompt = strip_slash_command_from_prompt(rendered_prompt, cmd)
            -- Load the skill content for later processing
            local content = skills.load_skill(skill_name)
            if content then
                table.insert(raw_skill_contents, content)
            end
        end
    end

    -- Append the cleaned skill contents.
    if #raw_skill_contents > 0 then
        rendered_prompt = rendered_prompt .. "\n" .. table.concat(raw_skill_contents, "\n")
    end

    local function has_in(what, command)
        -- in middle, between whitespace
        local found = what:find("%W(" .. command .. ")%W")
        -- start of string, with whitespace after
        found = found or what:find("^" .. command .. "%W")
        -- end of string, with whitespace before
        found = found or what:find("%W" .. command .. "$")
        return found ~= nil
    end
    local function prompt_has(command)
        return has_in(rendered_prompt, command)
    end

    ---@type ParseIncludesResult
    local includes = {
        all = (prompt == "") or prompt_has(M.slash_commands.ALL),
        yanks = prompt_has(M.slash_commands.YANKS),
        commits = prompt_has(M.slash_commands.COMMITS),
        current_file = prompt_has(M.slash_commands.FILE),
        open_files = prompt_has(M.slash_commands.OPEN_FILES),
        use_tools = prompt_has(M.slash_commands.TOOLS),
        readonly = prompt_has(M.slash_commands.READONLY),
        apply_template_only = prompt_has(M.slash_commands.TEMPLATE_ONLY),
        include_selection = prompt_has(M.slash_commands.SELECTION),
        top_k = top_k,
        rendered_prompt = "",
        norag = prompt_has(M.slash_commands.NORAG),
    }

    -- After processing skill content, propagate the effect of /all if it was discovered.
    if includes.all then
        includes.yanks = true
        includes.commits = true
        includes.current_file = true
        includes.open_files = true
    end

    for _, k in pairs(M.slash_commands) do
        rendered_prompt = strip_slash_command_from_prompt(rendered_prompt, k)
    end

    includes.rendered_prompt = rendered_prompt
    -- log:info("includes", vim.inspect(includes))
    return includes
end

## ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/predictions/context/init.lua:30-49
function CurrentContext:items(prompt, always_include)
    local items = {}
    local includes = prompts.parse_includes(prompt)

    -- allow override to force include context items
    always_include = always_include or {}
    includes.yanks = includes.yanks or (always_include.yanks == true)
    includes.commits = includes.commits or (always_include.commits == true)
    includes.matching_ctags = includes.matching_ctags or (always_include.matching_ctags == true)
    includes.project = includes.project or (always_include.project == true)

    if includes.yanks then
        items.yanks = yanks.get_context_item()
    end
    -- if includes.commits then
    --     items.commits = git_diff.get_context_items()
    -- end
    -- if includes.ctags then
    --     items.ctags = ctags.get_context_items()
    -- end

local prompts = require("ask-openai.predictions.context.prompts")
local skills = require("ask-openai.frontends.skills")

require('ask-openai.helpers.testing')

describe("parse_includes", function()
    describe("/all", function()
        local function should_detect_slash_all(original_prompt, expected_cleaned)
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes(original_prompt)
            assert.is_true(includes.all, "includes.all should be true for '" .. original_prompt .. "'")
            assert.are_equal(expected_cleaned, includes.rendered_prompt)
        end
        it("'/all' slash command is detected and stripped", function()
            should_detect_slash_all("foo /all bar", "foo bar")
            should_detect_slash_all("/all foo", "foo")
            should_detect_slash_all("foo /all", "foo")
        end)

        local function should_not_detect_slash_all(original_prompt)
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes(original_prompt)
            assert.is_false(includes.all, "includes.all should be false for '" .. original_prompt .. "'")
            assert.are_equal(original_prompt, includes.rendered_prompt)
        end
        it("'/all' alone is not detected - for now b/c that means theres no user prompt!", function()
            -- FYI maybe I want to have this parse and strip? but for now it seems moot to do this without any other prompt text
            should_not_detect_slash_all("/all")
        end)
        it("'/allFoo' - in front of word is not stripped", function()
            should_not_detect_slash_all("/allFoo ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" /allFoo ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" /allFoo")
            should_not_detect_slash_all("/allFoo")
        end)
        it("'foo/allbar' - in between word is not stripped", function()
            should_not_detect_slash_all("foo/allbar ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" foo/allbar ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" foo/allbar")
            should_not_detect_slash_all("foo/allbar")
        end)
        it("'bar/all' - at end of word is not stripped", function()
            should_not_detect_slash_all("bar/all ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" bar/all ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" bar/all")
            should_not_detect_slash_all("bar/all")
        end)
    end)
    describe("/others", function()
        it("should detect /yanks", function()
            function ensure_detects(command, field)
                field = field or command
                local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. command .. " bar")
                assert.is_true(includes[field], "includes." .. field .. " should be true")
                assert.are_equal("foo bar", includes.rendered_prompt)
            end

            -- just add one test per, do not exercise tests of the parsing/stripping
            ensure_detects("yanks")
            ensure_detects("commits")
            ensure_detects("file", "current_file")
            ensure_detects("WIP_open_files", "open_files")
            ensure_detects("selection", "include_selection")
        end)
    end)

    describe("skills", function()
        local fake_skill_name = "fake_skilly_poo"
        skills.cached_skill_commands = { fake_skill_name }
        -- skills._skill_paths[skill_name] = "/fake/skilly/poo" -- not required if injecting content into cache

        it("should detect and load skill commands", function()
            skills._skill_content_cache[fake_skill_name] = "INJECTED SKILLY POO"
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. fake_skill_name .. " bar")

response_message.reasoning_content:
We have a user request: "parameterize (pass "all" and have the test work) and then run tests of other static slash commands embedded in skill... i.e. yanks, commits"

We need to modify the tests (prompts.tests.lua) to parameterize passing a command name (e.g., "all") and have the test work. Also to add tests for other static slash commands embedded in skill content: yanks, commits. The user wants to preserve indentation, no markdown code fences, just code.

We need to modify prompts.tests.lua accordingly, preserving unrelated code/comments. The user wants to parameterize (pass "all" and have the test work). Likely they want to make a function that tests that if a slash command appears inside skill content, it gets recognized and affects the includes. Currently test for /all. We can make a helper function that takes the slash command name and field (maybe includes field name). Then test for /yanks and /commits.

Implementation: In the skills test suite, we can define a helper function ensure_skill_embedded_command_detects(command, include_field). Then set the skill content to "INJECTED SKILLY POO /" .. command, then parse includes with "foo /skillname bar". Then assert includes[include_field] is true and rendered_prompt matches "foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO". Also ensure that for "all", includes.all is true and the includes fields yanks, commits, etc. are true due to propagation? But test just need to confirm that includes.all is true. Already there is a test for /all in skill content. We can parameterize that to reuse same helper.

Thus modify the test file at appropriate lines. Let's locate the relevant part. The file has a describe("skills", ...) block with two tests: "should detect and load skill commands" and "should recursively resolve static slash commands within skill content". The second test checks /all detection. We'll replace that with a parameterized test for various commands.

Potential modifications:

- Add a local function test_skill_embedded_command(command, field) that sets skill content to "INJECTED SKILLY POO /" .. command, then parse includes, then assert includes[field] true (or for "all", also check includes.all true). Then assert rendered_prompt is "foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO". This function can be used for "all", "yanks", "commits". For "all" also check includes.all true.

- Then in test "should recursively resolve static slash commands within skill content", we can call test_skill_embedded_command for each command. Or we could rename test to "should recursively resolve static slash commands within skill content" and loop.

- Possibly also add separate tests for each command.

- Ensure we preserve indentation and comments.

Implementation details: The skill name is defined as fake_skill_name above. We'll reuse it.

We need to adjust the test to use the new parameterization.

Now check the code snippet:

```lua
it("should recursively resolve static slash commands within skill content", function()
    skills._skill_content_cache[fake_skill_name] = "INJECTED SKILLY POO /all"
    local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. fake_skill_name .. " bar")
    assert.is_true(includes.all, "includes.all should be true due to /all in skill content")
    assert.are_equal("foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO", includes.rendered_prompt)
    -- TODO test of all slash command embedded (LOOP?)
end)
```

We need to modify this test. Possibly rename to something like "should resolve static slash commands in skill content". Within we can have a helper local function test_slash_in_skill(command, field). Then call for "all", "yanks", "commits".

Also for "all", we also want to test that includes.all true and also includes.yanks etc are true (due to propagation). But test can just check includes.all true; also can check includes.yanks true etc.

Thus implement:

```
local function ensure_skill_embedded_command(command, field)
    field = field or command
    skills._skill_content_cache[fake_skill_name] = "INJECTED SKILLY POO /" .. command
    local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. fake_skill_name .. " bar")
    assert.is_true(includes[field], "includes." .. field .. " should be true due to /" .. command .. " in skill content")
    assert.are_equal("foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO", includes.rendered_prompt)
end
```

Then in test:

```
it("should resolve static slash commands embedded in skill content", function()
    ensure_skill_embedded_command("all")
    ensure_skill_embedded_command("yanks")
    ensure_skill_embedded_command("commits")
end)
```

Note: For "all", includes.all is true but also includes.yanks etc should be true because includes.all triggers propagation. The test above only checks includes[field] true (field defaults to command). For "all", includes.all will be true; but we might also want to test includes.yanks true after propagation. But the user only asked "run tests of other static slash commands embedded in skill... i.e. yanks, commits". So we can add those tests.

Also ensure that we preserve any TODO comments.

Potential modifications: The test for /all detection currently asserts includes.all true and rendered_prompt. We'll replace that with a parameterized version.

Implementation steps:

1. Find the describe("skills") block. Add helper function before tests (maybe inside the block but before tests). Could be after the previous test "should detect and load skill commands". Insert the helper function after that test, before the "should recursively resolve static slash commands within skill content" test.

2. Update the second test to use the helper and test multiple commands.

Potentially we can keep the test name "should recursively resolve static slash commands within skill content". It's fine.

Edge Cases: Ensure that skills._skill_content_cache is cleared or overwritten each time. It is overwritten in the helper.

Potential conflict: The function ensure_skill_embedded_command uses "field = field or command". For "all", includes.all is true; for "yanks", includes.yanks is true. It works.

But need to ensure that includes.all is recognized for "all". The parse_includes includes logic sets includes.all based on (prompt == "") or prompt_has(M.slash_commands.ALL). However after processing skill content, they propagate includes.all effect. In this test, the slash command /all appears in skill content. The code extracts skill content and appends it to rendered_prompt. Then includes are computed based on rendered_prompt after skill content appended? Let's see the parse_includes code. The flow:

- extract top_k.
- raw_skill_contents = {}
- rendered_prompt = prompt
- For each skill_name, detect skill command in rendered_prompt, remove it, load skill content, add to raw_skill_contents.
- Append raw_skill_contents to rendered_prompt after loop.
- Then define has_in and prompt_has which checks rendered_prompt (which includes skill content).
- includes = { all = (prompt == "") or prompt_has(M.slash_commands.ALL), ... }
- Then after includes.all, propagate includes for other fields.
- Then strip slash commands from rendered_prompt.
- includes.rendered_prompt = rendered_prompt.

Thus includes.all detection should find /all in skill content because after appending skill content, rendered_prompt includes the skill content "INJECTED SKILLY POO /all". Then includes.all will be true, and later they propagate yanks etc. Then they strip slash commands from rendered_prompt, removing the /all from the skill content as part of final rendered_prompt. That's why final rendered_prompt is "foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO". Good.

Thus our test for "yanks" will work similarly: skill content includes "/yanks". includes.yanks should be true.

One nuance: The slash commands defined in M.slash_commands. Let's check that file to know command names. The slash_commands probably contain "ALL", "YANKS", "COMMITS", etc. Let's open that file. Not shown, but from tests we see "yanks", "commits". They call ensure_detects("yanks") in earlier test. So slash commands are lower case? They likely define commands as lower-case strings. The detection uses M.slash_commands.YANKS etc. So our command strings "yanks" will match.

Thus implement accordingly.

Potential additional changes: The test for "should detect and load skill commands" already uses skill content injection and checks rendered_prompt includes skill content but not slash commands.

Now we need to preserve indentation. Use tabs? The file uses tabs (maybe spaces). Let's examine the file snippet. The file uses 4 spaces indentation typical. We'll follow the same.

Now incorporate code modifications accordingly.

Potential extra: Add a test for /commits as similar.

Potential other static slash commands: The user says "i.e. yanks, commits". So only those two needed.

Thus final modifications:

- Insert a helper function inside the "skills" describe block, after the first test, before second test.

- Update the second test to call ensure_skill_embedded_command for "all", "yanks", "commits". Possibly also test that includes.all true for /all and includes.yanks true for /yanks, includes.commits true for /commits.

- Keep the TODO comment.

Implementation details:

```
local function ensure_skill_embedded_command(command, field)
    field = field or command
    skills._skill_content_cache[fake_skill_name] = "INJECTED SKILLY POO /" .. command
    local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. fake_skill_name .. " bar")
    assert.is_true(includes[field], "includes." .. field .. " should be true due to /" .. command .. " in skill content")
    assert.are_equal("foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO", includes.rendered_prompt)
end
```

But we must also account for /all case where includes.all is true, but also includes.yanks etc may also be true. Our assert only checks includes[field] which for "all" is includes.all. That's fine.

Potentially we also want to test includes.yanks after /all, but not required.

Now we replace the test function:

```
it("should recursively resolve static slash commands within skill content", function()
    ensure_skill_embedded_command("all")
    ensure_skill_embedded_command("yanks")
    ensure_skill_embedded_command("commits")
    -- TODO test of all slash command embedded (LOOP?)
end)
```

Make sure that helper function is defined before test.

Potentially also rename test description accordingly. But not required.

local prompts = require("ask-openai.predictions.context.prompts")
local skills = require("ask-openai.frontends.skills")

require('ask-openai.helpers.testing')

describe("parse_includes", function()
    describe("/all", function()
        local function should_detect_slash_all(original_prompt, expected_cleaned)
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes(original_prompt)
            assert.is_true(includes.all, "includes.all should be true for '" .. original_prompt .. "'")
            assert.are_equal(expected_cleaned, includes.rendered_prompt)
        end
        it("'/all' slash command is detected and stripped", function()
            should_detect_slash_all("foo /all bar", "foo bar")
            should_detect_slash_all("/all foo", "foo")
            should_detect_slash_all("foo /all", "foo")
        end)

        local function should_not_detect_slash_all(original_prompt)
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes(original_prompt)
            assert.is_false(includes.all, "includes.all should be false for '" .. original_prompt .. "'")
            assert.are_equal(original_prompt, includes.rendered_prompt)
        end
        it("'/all' alone is not detected - for now b/c that means theres no user prompt!", function()
            -- FYI maybe I want to have this parse and strip? but for now it seems moot to do this without any other prompt text
            should_not_detect_slash_all("/all")
        end)
        it("'/allFoo' - in front of word is not stripped", function()
            should_not_detect_slash_all("/allFoo ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" /allFoo ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" /allFoo")
            should_not_detect_slash_all("/allFoo")
        end)
        it("'foo/allbar' - in between word is not stripped", function()
            should_not_detect_slash_all("foo/allbar ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" foo/allbar ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" foo/allbar")
            should_not_detect_slash_all("foo/allbar")
        end)
        it("'bar/all' - at end of word is not stripped", function()
            should_not_detect_slash_all("bar/all ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" bar/all ")
            should_not_detect_slash_all(" bar/all")
            should_not_detect_slash_all("bar/all")
        end)
    end)
    describe("/others", function()
        it("should detect /yanks", function()
            function ensure_detects(command, field)
                field = field or command
                local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. command .. " bar")
                assert.is_true(includes[field], "includes." .. field .. " should be true")
                assert.are_equal("foo bar", includes.rendered_prompt)
            end

            -- just add one test per, do not exercise tests of the parsing/stripping
            ensure_detects("yanks")
            ensure_detects("commits")
            ensure_detects("file", "current_file")
            ensure_detects("WIP_open_files", "open_files")
            ensure_detects("selection", "include_selection")
        end)
    end)

    describe("skills", function()
        local fake_skill_name = "fake_skilly_poo"
        skills.cached_skill_commands = { fake_skill_name }
        -- skills._skill_paths[skill_name] = "/fake/skilly/poo" -- not required if injecting content into cache

        it("should detect and load skill commands", function()
            skills._skill_content_cache[fake_skill_name] = "INJECTED SKILLY POO"
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. fake_skill_name .. " bar")
            assert.are_equal("foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO", includes.rendered_prompt)
        end)

        it("should recursively resolve static slash commands within skill content", function()
            skills._skill_content_cache[fake_skill_name] = "INJECTED SKILLY POO /all"
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. fake_skill_name .. " bar")
            assert.is_true(includes.all, "includes.all should be true due to /all in skill content")
            assert.are_equal("foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO", includes.rendered_prompt)
            -- TODO test of all slash command embedded (LOOP?)
        end)

        -- TODO test of embedded top_k
    end)
end)

        it("should detect and load skill commands", function()
            skills._skill_content_cache[fake_skill_name] = "INJECTED SKILLY POO"
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. fake_skill_name .. " bar")
            assert.are_equal("foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO", includes.rendered_prompt)
        end)

        local function ensure_skill_embedded_command(command, field)
            field = field or command
            skills._skill_content_cache[fake_skill_name] = "INJECTED SKILLY POO /" .. command
            local includes = prompts.parse_includes("foo /" .. fake_skill_name .. " bar")
            assert.is_true(includes[field], "includes." .. field .. " should be true due to /" .. command .. " in skill content")
            assert.are_equal("foo bar\nINJECTED SKILLY POO", includes.rendered_prompt)
        end

        it("should recursively resolve static slash commands within skill content", function()
            ensure_skill_embedded_command("all")
            ensure_skill_embedded_command("yanks")
            ensure_skill_embedded_command("commits")
            -- TODO test of all slash command embedded (LOOP?)
        end)
